items.title: Middle East live: US-Iran technical talks to restart next week, mediator Pakistan says
items.description: Technical discussions between the United States and Iran, which are being mediated by Pakistan, are to resume next week, a spokesman for Pakistan's foreign ministry told journalists in Islamabad on Wednesday. He said that the talks would presumably happen on Tuesday adding next Monday or Wednesday were also possible start dates, though there were no additional details on the location of the talks. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.

items.description: The police said the victim was abducted on 11 June in Ogbe Village, Obafemi Owode Local Government Area of Ogun State. The post Two suspected kidnappers killed in Ogun, victim rescued – Police appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Will Congress demanding US military halt action in Iran end the war?
items.description: The US Senate approved a war powers resolution seeking to block military action against Iran on Tuesday. But don't expect the war to end.

items.title: World Cup: Amiri another wildcard for Nagelsmann's Germany
items.description: The Mainz midfielder with Afghan roots has become a key member of Germany's 2026 World Cup squad. Coach Julian Nagelsmann said Amiri can provide the team with a spark when it's most needed.

items.title: UK's Starmer mulls 'political realities' after Burnham by-election
items.description: A member of UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Cabinet said it would be "delusional" to pretend there was no threat to his position. But he also said he had no information suggesting he might quit, as a newspaper reported.

items.title: U.S. and Iran conclude high-level talks in Switzerland, mediators say
items.description: A joint statement from mediating nations ​Qatar and Pakistan said the U.S. and Iran agreed to a roadmap toward a final deal within 60 days

items.title: France record heatwave sees 'hairdryer effect' threaten crops
items.description: Crops in the French region of Normandy are under threat from the latest heatwave. A combination of high soil temperatures with hot winds can see crops wither in hours - dubbed the ‘hairdryer effect’ - it now threatens the region's food production. FRANCE 24's Daniel Quinlan reports.

items.title: Manuel Adorni asset declaration shows wealth grew 45-fold in 2024
items.description: Cabinet chief’s documentation attempts to explain an astronomical rise in his net worth. His legal strategy and mounting complications in Congress. Leer más

items.title: Messi eyes World Cup record and birthday win against Austria
items.description: Albiceleste captain celebrates his birthday on Wednesday and he will hope to hold the World Cup scoring record outright by then. Leer más

items.title: Experts fired by Trump resurrect mothballed climate website
items.description: Fired US federal workers have revived a defunct climate website — pushing back as the Trump administration escalates cuts to publicly funded science and research.

items.title: Dogmatism, clumsiness force Milei off a winning wave
items.description: What can stop Milei? The answer is very simple: his programmatic extremism and political clumsiness. Leer más

items.title: Catholic Church urges Congress to reject Milei's property bill
items.description: Church bodies warn that Milei's government's 'Inviolability of Private Property' bill threatens national sovereignty over land, water and natural resources and call on lawmakers to vote it down. Leer más

items.title: Malaysia central bank fines AEON Credit for sanctions breaches
Congolese chefs put high-end spin on traditional cuisine
items.description: Yam gratin, safou-fruit butter and caterpillar sauce: in the Democratic Republic of Congo, a country not usually associated with high cuisine, a new generation of chefs is putting a gourmet spin on traditional recipes.

items.title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation
items.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation on Monday after less than two years in office in a term characterised by policy U-turns and deep public unpopularity

items.title: Iran war day 117: Nuclear inspections dispute as US Senate curbs war powers
items.description: Iran and the US clash over nuclear inspections and Hormuz as negotiators push for a final deal within 60 days.

items.title: Forty drown in France as people seek relief from heatwave
items.description: Meteorologists say the system ‌is creating a so-called heat dome, trapping hot air over western and central Europe and allowing temperatures to build day after day

items.title: U.S. House passes affordable housing bill, sending it to Trump for enactment
items.description: The high cost ​of living in the U.S., with the inflation rate rising significantly during President Trump’s second term in ⁠office, is ranked as a top worry by voters in ⁠public opinion polls.

items.title: Confirmed Ebola cases rise to 1003, including 254 deaths, DRC says
items.description: The number of confirmed Ebola cases in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has risen to 1003, including 254 deaths, authorities in the country said on Sunday.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Colombia battle past DR Congo to reach round of 32
items.description: ⁠Colombia secured ​their place in the World Cup knockouts round with a game to spare after a 1-0 victory over DR Congo pushed them back to the top of Group ​K ‌on Tuesday, with Daniel Munoz ⁠finding a winner in the 76th minute after the South Americans had been ‌frustrated for much of the evening.

items.title: African cities back pact to shape future of data centers
items.description: African cities are joining a global effort to shape the future of data centers. As demand for digital infrastructure surges, leaders are pushing for new rules to balance technological growth with energy and environmental concerns.

items.title: Rubio arrives in UAE with aim to head off Gulf Arab unease over tentative Iran deal
items.description: Mr. Rubio arrived in Abu Dhabi late on Tuesday (June 23, 2026) following a two-day flurry of diplomatic activity between the U.S. and Iran in Switzerland led by Vice President J.D. Vance

items.title: Algeria rallies past Jordan 2-1 for its first win at a World Cup since 2014
items.description: Algeria scored off two corner kicks in the second half to rally from an early deficit and defeat Jordan 2-1 on Monday to bolster its chances of advancing to the knockout round of the World Cup

items.title: Ukraine’s recovery to be deliberated in Poland amid Kyiv-Warsaw spat
items.description: Amid a brewing row that tests Poland's alliance with Ukraine, President Zelenskyy will skip a key conference.

items.title: Deadly bird flu discovered in second Australian state as virus found in SA
items.description: A third confirmed case of the deadly H5 bird flu has been found in a migratory bird on Australia's southern coast.

items.title: NT Labor MLA caught speeding multiple times, years after fatal crash conviction
items.description: Manuel Brown had his licence suspended for three months last October, which was not disclosed publicly at the time. The Arafura MLA also failed to disclose a conviction over a fatal crash when he was initially preselected for Labor in 2023.

items.title: NBA Draft 2026: Wizards select teen sensation AJ Dybantsa with No. 1 pick
items.description: The 6ft 8in (2.03m) 19-year-old forward was scooped by the Wizards after playing just one season of college basketball.

items.title: IMO, Oman announces plan to evacuate ships, 11,000 seafarers stranded in Persian Gulf
items.description: IMO says it has secured necessary safety guarantees, verified the conditions for safe navigation; operation involves cooperation with U.S., Iran, Oman, other coastal States in the region

items.title: Europe is spending billions to rearm, so why the delays?
items.description: European countries have been trying to scale up their military capabilities, but their current defense production and procurement model is not fit for purpose, say experts.

items.title: Samsung Electronics plans $59 billion share buyback, Yonhap reports
Southern Lebanon's displaced Shiites fear return of old divisions
items.description: Israel has ordered Shiite-majority communities to be emptied, accusing residents of supporting Hezbollah, while allowing Christian, Sunni and other residents to remain. That risks aggravating sectarian tensions that have been mostly dormant since Lebanon's 1982-2000 civil war.

items.title: Ukraine says major Crimea bridge destroyed in latest attack
items.description: Ukraine continues to hit infrastructure on the Crimea to isolate the Russian-annexed peninsula. Meanwhile, Russia attacked Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown with a ballistic missile in a deadly strike.

items.title: World Cup exploits of Maradona and Messi have Argentina fans in raptures
items.description: Fans buzzing after Lionel Messi bags another two goals in Argentina's second World Cup 2026 match. Leer más

items.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's likely next prime minister
items.description: Now that British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation, all eyes are on Andy Burnham. The former mayor of Manchester is seen as the favorite to succeed Starmer to lead the United Kingdom.

items.title: In the Ebola epicenter, a gold-mining town reacts with fear, disbelief and grit
items.description: NPR reports from Mongbwalu in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The fight to contain the virus faces obstacles from lack of supplies to residents who doubt that the virus is real.

items.title: Schweinsteiger's Ivory Coast comments spark racism debate
items.description: Bastian Schweinsteiger's TV analysis of Germany's World Cup opponents Ivory Coast appeared to draw on racist stereotypes. For some observers, the comments are evidence that racist tropes are still common in football.

items.title: Romania's Dan taps new PM, breaks democratic norms
items.description: President Nicusor Dan has nominated a new prime minister, without consulting Romania’s governing parties. Critics accuse Dan of breaking democratic rules.
